Title: A Scots pine 4-coumarate:CoA ligase with high cinnamic acid affinity is the primary candidate for pinosylvin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related stilbenoid contributing to pathogen resistance and heartwood decay resistance
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; 4CL with conserved Box I and Box II motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PsSTS and PsPMT2; elevated under stilbene-inducing conditions including wounding, heartwood formation/transition zone, and UV-C-related induction datasets
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression under stilbene-inducing conditions; expressed in tissues accumulating stilbenes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA and 4-coumaric acid to 4-coumaroyl-CoA using CoA and ATP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the critical/first committed step toward pinosylvin biosynthesis; prime candidate for the missing control point in the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pinosylvin biosynthesis by efficiently activating cinnamic acid; identified as the prime candidate for the last missing enzyme in the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC-DAD product identification; enzyme kinetics; site-directed mutagenesis; domain swapping/chimeric enzymes; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ps4CL2 was co-expressed with pinosylvin pathway genes PsSTS and PsPMT2 under stilbene-inducing conditions. 2. Ps4CL2 showed the highest catalytic efficiency toward cinnamic acid among tested 4CLs, driven by an unusually low Km. 3. Ps4CL2 is proposed as the primary cinnamate-activating enzyme and the final missing enzyme in Scots pine pinosylvin biosynthesis. 4. Its cinnamate preference depends on multidomain interactions rather than a single substrate-binding residue pair alone.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ps4CL2 did not discriminate strongly between cinnamic acid and 4-coumaric acid in vitro, but its exceptionally high affinity for cinnamate makes it physiologically suited for cinnamate activation at estimated cellular cinnamate concentrations. Final in planta proof by transgenic downregulation was not performed because it is technically challenging in Scots pine.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stilbene-inducing conditions including wounding, heartwood formation, and UV-C irradiation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value candidate for engineering or selecting enhanced pinosylvin production and wood decay resistance in pine; useful target for pathway reconstruction or gene stacking with STS and PMT2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome assemblies from prior Scots pine datasets (Lim et al., 2016; Lim et al., 2021; Paasela et al., 2023)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related stilbenoid contributing to pathogen resistance and heartwood decay resistance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis rather than pinosylvin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; 4CL with conserved Box I and Box II motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in stems of 5-year-old seedlings and in needles; expression pattern correlated with PsHCT and PsC3'H
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive in stem and needles; not co-expressed with PsSTS/PsPMT2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids, showing strong preference for 4-coumaric acid over cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for pinosylvin; likely functions mainly in lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Unlikely to be the main enzyme for pinosylvin biosynthesis; preferentially supports lignin-related phenylpropanoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC-DAD product identification; enzyme kinetics; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ps4CL1 was identified as a bona fide 4CL enzyme. 2. It showed highest specificity toward 4-coumaric acid among Scots pine 4CLs. 3. Its expression correlated with lignin-pathway genes PsHCT and PsC3'H rather than pinosylvin genes, arguing against a primary role in pinosylvin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ps4CL1 groups phylogenetically with lignin-related pine 4CLs; close homolog suppression in P. radiata causes weakly lignified tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wounding and heartwood formation datasets used for expression comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for manipulating lignification rather than pinosylvin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome assemblies from prior Scots pine datasets (Lim et al., 2016; Lim et al., 2021; Paasela et al., 2023)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential lignification effects inferred from homolog studies in pine species

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related stilbenoid contributing to pathogen resistance and heartwood decay resistance
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: likely flavonoid/secondary metabolite branch rather than pinosylvin-specific cinnamate activation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; 4CL with conserved Box I and Box II motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PsSTS and PsPMT2 under stilbene-inducing conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic and 4-coumaric acids to corresponding CoA thioesters, with preference for 4-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: can supply precursor pool but is not the main direct route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Co-expressed with pinosylvin genes but shows much lower affinity for cinnamic acid than Ps4CL2, making it a weaker candidate for pinosylvin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC-DAD product identification; enzyme kinetics; reciprocal mutagenesis; domain swapping/chimeric enzymes; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ps4CL3 was co-expressed with pinosylvin genes but had low catalytic efficiency toward cinnamic acid compared with Ps4CL2. 2. Reciprocal residue exchange and domain-swapping with Ps4CL2 showed that substrate preference differences involve multiple enzyme regions. 3. Ps4CL3 likely functions mainly outside the primary cinnamate-to-pinosylvin route.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ~90% amino acid similarity to Ps4CL2 made Ps4CL3 the key comparator for substrate-specificity dissection.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stilbene-inducing conditions including wounding, heartwood formation, and UV-C irradiation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative scaffold for protein engineering of cinnamate affinity in 4CL enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome assemblies from prior Scots pine datasets (Lim et al., 2016; Lim et al., 2021; Paasela et al., 2023)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related stilbenoid contributing to pathogen resistance and heartwood decay resistance
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: likely flavonoid/secondary metabolite branch rather than pinosylvin-specific cinnamate activation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; 4CL with conserved Box I and Box II motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PsSTS and PsPMT2 under stilbene-inducing conditions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic and 4-coumaric acids to corresponding CoA thioesters, with much stronger preference for 4-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: can supply precursor pool but is unlikely to be the primary direct enzyme for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Although co-expressed with pinosylvin genes, its kinetic preference for 4-coumaric acid argues against a primary role in pinosylvin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC-DAD product identification; enzyme kinetics; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ps4CL4 is a bona fide Scots pine 4CL and is co-expressed with pinosylvin genes. 2. It prefers 4-coumaric acid strongly over cinnamic acid. 3. Ps4CL4 is therefore unlikely to be the main cinnamate-activating enzyme for pinosylvin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clusters close to Ps4CL3/Pt4CL3-like enzymes and likely contributes to non-pinosylvin phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Stilbene-inducing conditions including wounding, heartwood formation, and UV-C irradiation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for redirecting hydroxycinnamate flux in secondary metabolism, but not the best candidate for pinosylvin engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/transcriptome assemblies from prior Scots pine datasets (Lim et al., 2016; Lim et al., 2021; Paasela et al., 2023)
